notes: DO NOT BELIEVE THE PLATINUM REPORTS. The game appears to be running perfectly, but there is a bug where in some levels there are sprites that are not rendered, sometimes making progress very difficult. The bug is documented here: https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=30550.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: Sound would only play in videos. But aside from that is runs very well.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: There are some levels (happened at the first bridge) where the sprites platform sprites where completely invisible. Still playable, but no platforms. Other than that the game runs perfectly
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: Perfect running (video and audio) out of the box on Proton 4.2-3!
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: It works absolutely perfect for me using Proton 4.2 and enabling d9vk012 (instead of galliumnine) with protontricks. With galliumnine, some sprites were still broken for me. Using d9vk, I'm getting perfect gameplay just as I would using Windows.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes:
You must use D9VK with Proton to prevent "invisible" textures ( PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command% ). "Over ther Rainbow" level from the 1st world is a good one to see the issue. Another good level, much later though, is "Swimming with Stars" from the sea world. Eel-like creatures have only heads, bodies are invisible without D9VK. Rare minor visual artifacts are still present, but I name them rare and minor for a reason.

Lol, I thought that was a feature of the game, but that appeared to be a bug of Wine or whatever. My struggles will end now :D

Other than that Rayman Origins runs perfectly, highly recommend for family playthrough!
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: I've tested this before, but I've since switched my distro, replaced my CPU/Motherboard, and would like to report my new findings. If you have an AMD GPU, the game runs absolutely perfectly using D9vk. If you have a Nvidia GPU, it still works pretty well using the proprietary drivers and D9vk, but there are some very minor graphical glitches in certain areas; mainly the trails that lead to the cages having small breaks in them, though it won't affect the actual gameplay..
title: Rayman® Origins

notes:
D9vk needs to be used otherwise some levels are not playable because of missing graphics elements (fire, waterfalls ...). However there still remain minor graphic glitches. But it is not a big problem, it's almost unnoticeable.
Game performs very well even on this weak integrated GPU.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes:
As of 4.11-4, the majority of issues with sprites rendering correctly in the game have been resolved. There are still very slight issues, such as the air currents not rendering correctly on the Desert of Dijiridoos, though this is only a minor issue. This is great for NVIDIA users, as it works even better than D9vk did for those GPU's.

D9vk with an AMD GPU still works even better than the latest proton when it comes to the sprite rendering, but as of proton 4.11-3, the game won't launch with D9vk, meaning you'll need revert to 4.2-9 instead if you want to use it.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: In addition to my last report, you can actually use D9vk in proton 4.11-4 by setting the launch options under Steam to "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%". I recommend using D9vk if you have an AMD GPU, or leaving it disabled to use OpenGL if you have a NVIDIA GPU.
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: I have no issue's with this game. I posted a steamcommunity video at: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=03M-KQaXBeY&feature=youtu.be
title: Rayman® Origins

notes: I recently found out about dgVoodoo. Using dgVoodoo and DXVK in the game makes the sprites render correctly, so it also works properly on NVIDIA. I tested it with a GTX970 and a VEGA64, it seems to work flawlessly on both.
title: Rayman® Origins
